"visible-to-user": false, "enabled": true, "bounds": [88, 224, 1352, -425], "pressed": "not_pressed"}, {"text": "Trenìt! is the most popular app to search trains schedules and prices in Italy, available for both Android and iOS.

Its first version was released in September 2009 with the name Italian Trains Timetable, and it became Trenìt! in May 2014.
  
Main features:
 ⚫ search Trenitalia, Italo, Trenord and many other trains
 ⚫ detailed infos about trains status
 ⚫ complete view of all types of available tickets
 ⚫ possible to sort results by time, price and duration of the trip
 ⚫ direct links to purchase tickets directly from the train companies
 ⚫ list of all stops, with arrival and departure times
 ⚫ map of the journey with all stops
 ⚫ optimized for tablets (with dual-pane in landscape)
 ⚫ possible to share the details of a trip (via email, Facebook, Whatsapp, etc.)
 ⚫ app available in multiple languages", "resource-id": "eu.baroncelli.oraritrenitalia:id/trenit", "adapter-view": false, "pointer": "e4b7471", "scrollable-horizontal": false, "ancestors": ["android.widget.TextView", "android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "selected": false, "content-desc": [null], "rel-bounds": [88, 208, 1352, 1868], "draw": false, "focusable": false, "long-clickable": false, "visibility": "visible", "focused": false, "clickable": false, "abs-pos": true, "font-family": "sans-serif", "class": "android.support.v7.widget.AppCompatTextView", "visible-to-user": true, "package": "eu.baroncelli.oraritrenitalia", "enabled": true, "bounds": [88, 224, 1352, 1269], "pressed": "not_pressed", "scrollable-vertical": false}, {"text": "About the author", "scrollable-vertical": false, "adapter-view": false, "pointer": "4013256", "scrollable-horizontal": false, "ancestors": ["android.widget.TextView", "android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "selected": false, "content-desc": [null], "rel-bounds": [88, 1956, 1352, 2041], "draw": false, "focusable": false, "long-clickable": false, "visibility": "visible", "focused": false, "clickable": false, "abs-pos": true, "font-family": "sans-serif", "class": "android.support.v7.widget.AppCompatTextView", "visible-to-user": true, "enabled": true, "bounds": [88, 1357, 1352, 1442], "pressed": "not_pressed"}, {"text": "Daniele Baroncelli is an internet and mobile architect expert. Born in Florence (Italy), he studied MEng Electronic & Communication Engineering at the University of Bath (UK) and received a Master’s degree in Internet Engineering from the University of Florence.
Among his projects, in 1997 he founded the biggest music portal in Italy Rockit.It, and in 2005 the biggest portal about Italian cinema CinemaItaliano.info.  ⚫ Russian (thanks to Dmitry Matyukhin!)
 ⚫ French (thanks to Etienne Mansard!)
 ⚫ German (thanks to Maria Neumayer!)

 ⚫ Portoguese BRA (thanks to Gabriela Reis!)
 ⚫ Korean (thanks to TaeKyu Kim!)
 ⚫ Romanian (thanks to Mihai Costel Carpea!)
 ⚫ Hungarian (thanks to József Barna!)
 ⚫ Turkish (thanks to Şevket Yilmaz!)

The language shown to the user depends on the general settings of the device. talented French designer, who gave his neat and fresh touch to the app
 ⚫ Mark Allison (London):
my Android guru, used to work with him, recently nominated GDE (Google Developer Expert) by Google
 ⚫ Maria Neumayer (San Francisco):
my Silicon Valley insider, she knows everything about the coolest tools and libraries
 ⚫ Bill Kennedy (Miami):
the most passionate Go developer you can meet, as well as author of the book Go In Action, released in November 2015
possibly the best developer I met in my career, he can always provide the smartest possible solutions
 ⚫ Dmitry Matyukhin (London):
possibly the best developer I met in my career, he can always provide the smartest possible solutions
 ⚫ Rob Sproats (London):
the most knowledgable IT enthusiast, he can answer 99% of any kind of question
